message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background and Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We conducted a single center cross-sectional study to investigate racial disparities in the hesitancy and utilization of monoclonal antibody (mAb) treatment of COVID-19 among treatment eligible patients who were referred to the infusion center between January 4, 2021 and May 14, 2021.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Among the 2,406 eligible participants, African Americans were significantly more likely to underutilize mAb treatment (OR 1.8; 95% CI 1.5-2.1) and miss treatment opportunities due to monoclonal hesitancy (OR 1.7, 95% CI 1.3-2.1).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Addressing racial disparities in mAb delivery is an opportunity to bridge the racial inequities in COVID-19 care.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Racial Disparities in Hesitancy and Utilization of Monoclonal Antibody Infusion Treatment of COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Nguyen LH , Joshi AD , Drew DA , et al. Racial and ethnic differences in COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy and uptake. medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences 2021.

reference.article-title: Achieving Racial and Ethnic Equity in COVID-19 Vaccination
